defects.finding: Security invariants were distributed across runtime, launchers and package scripts without one fail-closed M7 gate.
defects.closure: Added a repository audit that blocks implicit attestations, marketplace writes, shell=True, unapproved network-write primitives and missing package/Defender/path controls.

defects.finding: No candidate-versus-baseline performance comparison was pinned to the M6 exact head.
defects.closure: Added a median-based adapter normalization and registry-resolution probe against immutable M6 head 0972d02312bdee3aae3583dc6d9caf369622a57d with ratio and absolute-slack gates.

defects.finding: One-click security, error and reproducibility evidence was spread across earlier workflows rather than enforced by one M7 exact-head workflow.
defects.closure: Added a dedicated Windows M7 job that parses PowerShell, runs repository contracts and executes the existing native one-button Red Team on the exact PR head.

defects.finding: User-facing one-click failures had no explicit regression contract for actionable messages.
defects.closure: The M7 audit requires concrete messages for missing Python, missing non-interactive source, invalid configuration, cancelled selection and missing result output.

defects.finding: A security or one-click gate could produce evidence for a commit other than the current PR head.
defects.closure: Every M7 job checks out and asserts the exact candidate SHA; the performance job separately asserts the immutable M6 baseline SHA.

defects.finding: The first exact-head M7 audit classified a forwarded, already confirmed attestation, a CI forbidden-marker string and OSS admission URL reading as runtime authorization or marketplace/network writes.
defects.closure: Replaced substring classification with PowerShell switch-default detection and Python AST assignment analysis; narrowed network findings to explicit HTTP write verbs. Added precision regression tests. Production runtime and launcher behavior were not changed.

defects.finding: The duplicated Windows focused-test orchestration failed while the canonical full Windows baseline on the same exact head passed.
defects.closure: Removed the divergent hand-selected module invocation from the M7 Windows job and delegated to the repository-owned quantum.scripts.ci entry point before the native one-button Red Team.

defects.finding: The dedicated M7 Windows job did not reproduce the canonical Windows baseline environment: Git CRLF conversion changed immutable bytes and the pinned tzdata dependency was absent.
defects.closure: The job now disables core.autocrlf before checkout, installs exactly tzdata 2026.2 as declared by pyproject.toml, preserves the full contract log and then runs the native one-button Red Team.
